nodes.label: Auth Redirects And API Auth Errors

nodes.label: CSV Transaction Import Feature
nodes.source_location: CSV transaction import with column mapping and account assignment

nodes.label: Dashboard Analytics
nodes.source_location: Dashboard analytics with KPI cards, period comparisons, category breakdowns, and daily charts

nodes.label: Milliunit Amount Storage Convention
nodes.source_location: Transaction amounts are stored in milliunits.
nodes.rationale: Amounts stored as integer milliunits avoid floating-point precision errors in financial calculations.

nodes.label: Transaction Ownership Validation Gap (P0)
nodes.source_location: ### P0 - Enforce ownership validation for transaction create and bulk-create
nodes.rationale: Create and bulk-create routes insert without checking that the submitted accountId/categoryId belong to the authenticated user, enabling an auth bypass.

nodes.label: CSV Import Validation Hardening (P0)
nodes.source_location: ### P0 - Harden CSV import validation and fail closed on malformed input
nodes.rationale: Import flow trusts parsed rows too much; any types, missing row schema validation, and unchecked date/amount conversion can produce invalid payloads.

nodes.label: CSRF Protection for Mutating Endpoints (P0)
nodes.source_location: ### P0 - Add CSRF protection for mutating endpoints
nodes.rationale: No CSRF middleware or token verification exists, leaving all authenticated mutations open to cross-site request forgery.

nodes.label: Rate Limiting on Mutating APIs (P0)
nodes.source_location: ### P0 - Add rate limiting on mutating APIs
nodes.rationale: Write-heavy endpoints are unthrottled; no rate limiting middleware exists on any mutating route.

nodes.label: Summary Date Param Strict Validation (P1)
nodes.source_location: ### P1 - Strictly validate summary date params
nodes.rationale: summary.ts accepts arbitrary date strings and uses parse() without rejecting invalid dates, allowing incorrect analytics windows.

nodes.label: Debug Summary Endpoint Removal (P1)
nodes.rationale: .get('/asd', ...) remains in summary.ts, exposing undocumented production surface area.

nodes.label: No Automated Test Coverage (P1)
nodes.source_location: ### P1 - Start automated coverage for critical money paths
nodes.rationale: No test files in repository; every fix ships with regression risk.

nodes.label: Soft Deletes for Accounts and Categories (P2)
nodes.source_location: ### P2 - Introduce soft deletes / restore flows
nodes.rationale: Schema has no deleted_at or restore path, making accidental deletes irreversible.
nodes.norm_label: soft deletes for accounts and categories (p2)

nodes.label: Client-First Import Workflow Rationale
nodes.source_location: ### Why This Architecture? - 1. Client-first import workflow
nodes.rationale: Mapping and normalization happen before mutation submission to give users immediate control and validation feedback, reusing existing bulk-create APIs to minimize backend scope.

nodes.label: Tech Debt Ticket Generation Agent Instructions
nodes.norm_label: tech debt ticket generation agent instructions

nodes.label: Copilot PR Review Prompt Agent Instructions
nodes.norm_label: copilot pr review prompt agent instructions

nodes.label: Client-to-Database Request Flow
nodes.rationale: Page -> TanStack Query hook -> typed Hono RPC client -> Hono handler (Clerk auth + Drizzle query) -> normalized JSON response. Milliunit-to-display conversions happen in the hook.

nodes.label: Import Review, Normalization, and Deduplication (Roadmap Feature 2)
nodes.norm_label: import review, normalization, and deduplication (roadmap feature 2)

nodes.label: Transfers, Balances, and Net Worth (Roadmap Feature 3)
nodes.norm_label: transfers, balances, and net worth (roadmap feature 3)

nodes.label: External Parser Service (PDF/CSV to Transactions)
nodes.source_location: A separate parser service is being built to convert PDFs and CSVs into transaction-shaped data

nodes.label: node-postgres Drizzle Runtime Switch
nodes.source_location: Switched Drizzle runtime from Neon HTTP to node-postgres via db/connection.ts
nodes.rationale: Switching from Neon HTTP adapter to node-postgres enables local Docker Postgres compatibility and richer connection pooling control.

nodes.label: Remote Postgres TLS Certificate Verification
nodes.source_location: Remote Postgres TLS now verifies certificates by default
nodes.rationale: Default TLS verification prevents man-in-the-middle on remote DB connections; ALLOW_INSECURE_DATABASE_TLS=true allows opt-out for known self-signed scenarios.

nodes.label: globalThis Drizzle Pool HMR Cache
nodes.source_location: App Drizzle pool creation uses a globalThis cache outside production to avoid Next.js dev HMR pool multiplication
nodes.rationale: Next.js HMR reloads modules on each change; without a globalThis cache each reload would open a new pg.Pool, exhausting connections during development.

nodes.label: CSV Import Chunked Bulk-Create Submission
nodes.source_location: CSV transaction imports are chunked into the same batch size accepted by the bulk-create endpoint

nodes.label: Inclusive End-of-Day Date Filter Fix
nodes.source_location: to=yyyy-MM-dd filters are inclusive through the end of that calendar day
nodes.rationale: Without end-of-day inclusivity, newly created same-day transactions are hidden by date filters, causing user confusion.

links.context: SVG shows a document shape with a folded top-right corner and three horizontal lines representing text, a classic file/document metaphor
Gray (#666) fill, minimal 16x16 grid design consistent with toolbar/UI icon convention

links.note: SVG named vercel.svg placed in the public/ directory is used as branding/attribution for the Vercel platform, likely shown in the app UI or footer.
The SVG file contains a single white triangle path (d='m577.3 0 577.4 1000H0z') on a transparent background, representing the Vercel logo mark.
The triangle is the recognized brand mark for Vercel, the deployment and hosting platform.

links.context: The SVG file contains the Next.js wordmark/logo rendered in black, depicting the text NEXT.JS in stylized lettering
The logo SVG is stored in the public assets directory of the Nuchi project

window.svg resides in the public/ assets directory

links.rationale: SVG logo file in public/ directory represents the brand identity of the Nuchi application
